title: Type Inference and Dynamic
description: How Dart infers types and when to use dynamic.

slides.title: Type Inference 🔍
slides.content:
# var vs dynamic

**`var`** – Dart infers the type from the initial value. Once inferred, the type is fixed.

**`dynamic`** – Turns off type checking; can hold any type and change type at runtime.

**`Object?`** – Also can hold anything, but requires casting to call specific methods.

Best practice: Use `var` or explicit types; avoid `dynamic` unless interoperating with dynamic data (e.g., JSON).

quizData.question: What happens if you declare `var x = 'hello';` and later try `x = 42;`?

options.text: It works, because var is dynamic.
Compile error: can't assign int to String.

quizData.explanation: `var` infers the type from the initializer; here `x` is inferred as `String`, so assigning an `int` causes a compile error.

slides.title: See it in Action
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Prefer `Object?` over `dynamic` if you need flexibility but want some type safety

`Object?` at least reminds you to cast, while `dynamic` disables all checks.
